data.title: Polymarket Odds of the Clarity Act Becoming Law in 2026 Fall to 39% Amid Senate Debate
data.description:
Sen. Cynthia Lummis defended the Clarity Act, saying it includes more than 16 safeguards aimed at addressing illicit finance risks.

According to NS3.AI, Sen. Elizabeth Warren argued that the digital-asset bill would weaken existing standards.

Market expectations on the bill’s prospects also shifted, with Polymarket placing the odds of the Clarity Act becoming law in 2026 at 39%, down from earlier levels.

data.title: STOCKS | Carry Wealth Holdings Says Court Order Seeks to Restrict Transfer of 411 Million Shares
data.description:
Carry Wealth Holdings (0643) said it received an originating summons stop notice issued by the High Court on June 30 this year, according to Ming Pao.

The company said the notice was filed by applicant Giant Fortune International Development and seeks to restrict the transfer of 411 million shares currently registered under MARS Worldwide, as well as the payment of any dividends or interest on those shares, until 14 days’ notice has been given to Giant Fortune’s legal representative.

data.title: STOCKS | Leapmotor Chairman Zhu Jiangming and Shareholder Fu Liquan Buy 4.81M H Shares
data.description:
Leapmotor (9863) said shareholder, chairman and CEO Zhu Jiangming and shareholder Fu Liquan bought a combined 4.81 million H shares in the market at an average price of about HK$35.86 per share, according to Ming Pao.

The purchases involved total consideration of about HK$173 million, the company said in an announcement.

data.title: Swiss National Bank Says Federal Council Proposal Targets Risks, Mainly Affecting UBS Group
data.description:
The Swiss National Bank said a proposal by the Federal Council provided targeted responses to the risks in question and would mainly affect UBS Group.

According to Jin10, the central bank said the Federal Council’s proposal was designed to address those risks in a focused way, with the primary impact falling on UBS Group.

data.title: STOCKS | India Textile Stocks Stand Out on Trade Deals
data.description:
Shares of Indian textile makers supplying T-shirts, bed linen and towels to global retailers including Walmart Inc. have been among this year’s biggest stock-market winners, according to Bloomberg.

Some investors are betting the rally has further to run, helped by trade deals.

data.title: Vietnam Central Bank Pledges to Support Growth Despite Rising Price Pressures
data.description: Vietnam’s central bank said it will try to balance support for economic growth with efforts to contain price pressures that are expected to intensify through the end of 2026, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Polymarket Odds Rise to 64% That GPT-5.6 Will Open to the Public on July 7
data.description:
Polymarket’s probability that “GPT-5.6 will open to the public on July 7” rose to 64%, up 26% over the past 24 hours. According to Odaily, the market’s rules define GPT-5.6 as a model explicitly named “GPT-5.6” or a model officially recognized as a direct successor to GPT-5.5, including versions such as GPT-5.7 or GPT-5.8.

The rules state that task-specific models such as GPT-Codex and Transcribe, cost-optimized versions such as Nano and Mini, and the o-series reasoning models are included in the market, while GPT-6 or other next-generation flagship models are excluded.

To qualify, the model must be made available to the public, including through a public beta or an open waitlist. Closed testing or private-only access does not meet the requirement. Settlement will primarily rely on OpenAI’s official announcements and publicly available information on its website, with verification based on mainstream media reports.

Odaily reported that on June 27, OpenAI introduced its next-generation GPT-5.6 series models, which are currently in a limited preview for a small number of partners.

data.title: Pakistan Says Next U.S.-Iran Talks Expected After Khamenei Funeral
data.description:
Pakistan’s Foreign Ministry said the next round of U.S.-Iran talks was expected to be held soon after the funeral ceremony of Iran’s former supreme leader, Khamenei, ends.

According to Jin10, the ministry said in a July 2 statement that Qatar and Pakistan, acting as mediators, held separate meetings in Doha with negotiating representatives from the United States and Iran.

The statement said the discussions focused on implementing issues related to a U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding. It added that the parties held in-depth talks on several specific matters covered by the memorandum and made “positive progress” on related topics.

Pakistan’s Foreign Ministry said the parties agreed to continue negotiations over the coming period and to determine the timing of the next round of talks as soon as possible.

data.title: OpenAI Discusses Giving US Government a 5% Stake, FT Reports
data.description: OpenAI has begun discussions about giving the US government a 5% stake in the ChatGPT developer, the Financial Times reported, citing two people familiar with the talks, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Business Jet Departures in Middle East Drop About 30% After Iran War
data.description: Business jet departures in the Middle East fell about 30% in the aftermath of the Iran war, and the decline risks persisting until a peace deal is signed, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Asia-Pacific Deal Volume Tops $750 Billion in 2026 Despite Global Disruption
data.description: Deal volume in and out of the Asia-Pacific region has already topped $750 billion in 2026, despite disruption in parts of the world, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Vietnam, Philippines Raised to Upper-Middle Income Status
data.description: The World Bank raised Vietnam and the Philippines to upper-middle income status after years of brisk economic expansion, potentially boosting investor confidence in both countries, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Offers 5% Equity Stake to Trump Administration, Report Says
data.description:
OpenAI has proposed providing a 5% equity stake to the Trump administration, the Financial Times reported.

According to Jin10, the report did not provide additional details on the terms or timing of the proposal.

data.title: QDII Approved Quota Totals $176.17 Billion, SAFE Data Show
data.description:
China’s cumulative approved investment quota under the Qualified Domestic Institutional Investor (QDII) program totaled $176.17 billion as of the end of June, according to the latest disclosure from the State Administration of Foreign Exchange.

According to Jin10, the QDII quota approval table showed that securities and fund institutions accounted for $97.28 billion of the cumulative approved quota.

The data also indicated that the most recent QDII quota allocation was issued on March 23.

data.title: Japan 10-Year Bond Sale Draws Weaker Demand Than 12-Month Average
data.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond auction drew weaker demand than the average for the past year amid concerns the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Citi’s Luet Says China Franchise Has Grown Strongly
data.description:
Marc Luet, Citi’s head of Japan, Asia North & Australia and banking, said the bank has seen the role of the yuan grow and is hopeful of obtaining a securities license in China, according to Bloomberg.

Luet made the comments in an exclusive interview on “Bloomberg: The China Show.”

data.title: Binance to Suspend Injective Network Deposits and Withdrawals for INJ Upgrade and Hard Fork
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the exchange will suspend deposits and withdrawals of token(s) on the Injective (INJ) network starting at approximately 2026-07-02 13:00 (UTC) to support a planned network upgrade and hard fork. Binance said the suspension is intended to facilitate the upgrade process and that it will manage the technical requirements involved for users during the event. The network upgrade and hard fork are scheduled to take place at the block height of 172,502,000, or approximately at 2026-07-02 14:00 (UTC). Binance added that trading of token(s) on the Injective network will not be impacted during the deposit and withdrawal pause.

Service Status and Resumption: Binance said deposits and withdrawals for token(s) on the Injective (INJ) network will be reopened once the upgraded network is deemed stable. The exchange stated that no further announcement will be posted regarding the reopening. Upgrade Timing Details: The deposit and withdrawal suspension is set to begin at approximately 2026-07-02 13:00 (UTC), ahead of the upgrade and hard fork expected around 2026-07-02 14:00 (UTC) at block height 172,502,000. Trading Impact: Binance said trading on the aforementioned network will not be affected while deposits and withdrawals are suspended.

data.title: Kosdaq Tech-Track Firms Face Delisting Review After Major Business Changes Within Five Years
data.description:
KRX has amended its listing rules so Kosdaq tech-track companies that change their main business within five years will be subject to a substantive delisting review.

According to NS3.AI, the revised rule is intended to address cases where listed firms pivot into new activities, including managing virtual asset treasuries.

The amendment excludes business changes that are considered similar to, or ancillary to, a company’s existing operations.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Hong Kong-Listed Gold Stocks Extend Gains, China Gold International Up Over 13%
data.description:
Hong Kong-listed gold stocks continued to strengthen, led by China Gold International (02099.HK), which rose more than 13%.

According to Jin10, Lingbao Gold (03330.HK) gained more than 11%, while Chifeng Gold (06693.HK) and Zijin Gold International (02259.HK) also moved higher.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Musk Says Optimus Mass Production Will Start Extremely Slowly
data.description:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the company’s Optimus humanoid robot would begin mass production at an “extremely slow” pace because the underlying technology is newly developed.

According to Jin10, Musk made the comment after posting on social media a photo of himself at an Optimus production line at Tesla’s Fremont factory in California, prompting renewed discussion about the robot’s manufacturing progress.

A social media user suggested Tesla had recently reduced public demonstrations of Optimus, possibly because mass production was advancing faster than market expectations. Musk rejected that interpretation, saying the initial production phase would be very slow and emphasizing that building Optimus is not comparable to manufacturing cars.
